phase_0.notes: All source files (38 .gs + 19 .html/.js = 57 files + 1 extra) read successfully. Config and skill reference files all loaded. Analysis based on static code review only - no runtime testing performed.

tech_debt_findings.title: File exceeds recommended size threshold (1,796 lines)
tech_debt_findings.description: 21_AliasService.gs is the largest file in Group 1 at 1,796 lines. While well-organized with clear sections, this exceeds the 1,000-line maintainability guideline and makes code navigation difficult.
tech_debt_findings.evidence: File: 21_AliasService.gs | Total Lines: ~1,796 | Functions: ~25+ | Sections: Cache, CRUD, Import, Lookup, Migration
tech_debt_findings.fix_suggestion: Consider splitting into: 21_AliasCache.gs (cache operations), 21_AliasCrud.gs (CRUD), 21_AliasImport.gs (bulk import/migration). Pattern already established by 10b/10d/10e/10f/10g/10h split from original 10_MatchEngine.

tech_debt_findings.title: God function: runPipelineBatch_() exceeds 150 lines
tech_debt_findings.description: Main pipeline orchestration function runPipelineBatch_() is approximately 150 lines with multiple responsibilities including state management, quota checking, batch processing loop, error handling, and notification.
tech_debt_findings.fix_suggestion:
Extract sub-functions following existing pattern:
- initPipelineRun_(opts) → state object
- shouldContinuePipeline_(state, stats) → boolean
- finalizePipelineRun_(state, stats) → result
Pattern: See 10_MatchEngine.runMatchEngine_() → runPipelineWithCheckpoint_()

tech_debt_findings.title: God function: applyReviewDecision() at ~162 lines
tech_debt_findings.description: Core review decision function handles multiple responsibilities: validation, decision parsing, merge/create/escalate dispatch, status update, audit logging, cache invalidation, and notification.
tech_debt_findings.fix_suggestion:
Extract following existing pattern from 10g_MatchRowProcessor.gs:
- validateReviewInput_(reviewId, decision) → validated obj
- executeDecision_(decision, context) → dispatcher
- persistDecisionResult_(result) → sheet write + cache invalidate
Target: Each helper < 50 lines

tech_debt_findings.title: God function: fetchDataFromSCGJWD() estimated ~190 lines
tech_debt_findings.description: SCG data fetch function handles URL construction, cookie management, API call with retry, response parsing, data transformation, validation, and sheet writing in a single function.
tech_debt_findings.fix_suggestion:
Split into:
- buildScgRequestOptions_() → fetch options
- parseScgResponse_(rawBody) → structured data
- transformToDailyJobFormat_(rawData) → normalized rows
- writeDailyJobBatch_(rows) → sheet write with chunking

tech_debt_findings.title: Function exceeds 100 lines: runTestMatchDryRun_() at ~213 lines
tech_debt_findings.description: Dry-run test harness function processes rows with time guard, builds test results, formats output, and writes to TEST_MATCH_RESULTS sheet. Exceeds 100-line SRP guideline.
tech_debt_findings.fix_suggestion:
Extract:
- collectDryRunResults_(rows) → array of results
- formatDryRunOutput_(results) → formatted rows for sheet
- writeDryRunResults_(formattedRows) → sheet write
Keep orchestrator under 80 lines

tech_debt_findings.title: Function exceeds 100 lines: resolveAndPersistMerge_() at ~158 lines
tech_debt_findings.description: MERGE path handler for resolved matches creates/updates person, place, geo, destination records plus alias enrichment and semantic notes. Complex but cohesive - borderline acceptable.
tech_debt_findings.fix_suggestion:
Consider extracting:
- persistMasterRecordsMerge_(row, ctx) → {person, place, geo}
- enrichMergeAliases_(personId, placeId, ctx) → alias creation
Current size is acceptable if further split harms readability

tech_debt_findings.title: Hardcoded Thailand geographic bounds as magic numbers
tech_debt_findings.description: Thailand bounding box coordinates (5.5, 20.5, 97.5, 105.7) are hardcoded inline rather than defined as named constants. While geographically specific, these should be configurable for potential expansion.

tech_debt_findings.title: Magic threshold values for Dice coefficient matching
tech_debt_findings.description: Tie-breaker thresholds TIE_BREAK_THRESHOLD (0.7) and NEAR_MISS_THRESHOLD (0.4) are hardcoded as local variables instead of being configurable via AI_CONFIG.
tech_debt_findings.fix_suggestion:
Add to AI_CONFIG in 01_Config.gs:
TIE_BREAK_SCORE: 0.7,    // Dice coeff for tie-break
NEAR_MISS_SCORE: 0.4,     // Dice coeff for near-miss
This allows tuning without code deployment

tech_debt_findings.title: Code duplication: Cache invalidation logic repeated
tech_debt_findings.description: Cache invalidation loop (iterate chunks, remove keys) appears in both invalidateSourceCache() (L386-399) and updateSyncStatus_() (L488-502). Same pattern with minor variation.

tech_debt_findings.title: Silent catch blocks may hide failures
tech_debt_findings.description: Some catch blocks in review service silently ignore JSON parse errors. While intentional for optional candidate data, this could mask real data corruption issues.

tech_debt_findings.title: Potential XSS in Leaflet popup HTML injection
tech_debt_findings.description: Map marker popup binds data directly into HTML string without escaping. If matchStatus or count fields contain HTML, it could be executed in browser context.
tech_debt_findings.fix_suggestion:
Use escapeHtml() from ViewHelpers: marker.bindPopup('' + p.count + ' deliveries
' + 'Status: ' + escapeHtml(p.matchStatus) + '
' + ...); ViewHelpers.escapeHtml() is already available and used elsewhere

tech_debt_findings.title: Unescaped error message in innerHTML assignment
tech_debt_findings.description: Error message from server is inserted directly into DOM via innerHTML without HTML escaping. Low risk (internal errors) but violates defense-in-depth principle.
tech_debt_findings.fix_suggestion:
Apply ViewHelpers.escapeHtml(): container.innerHTML = '
' + 'Error: ' + escapeHtml(errorMessage) + '
'; Consistent with other views (FactDelivery, SourceSheet, Search)

tech_debt_findings.title: Debug log statements in production auth function
tech_debt_findings.description: isAuthorizedDashboardUser_() contains logInfo statements that emit effective user email to SYS_LOG on every WebApp access. While useful for debugging, this creates log volume and PII exposure.

tech_debt_findings.title: Deprecated compatibility layer still present
tech_debt_findings.description: 99_Legacy.gs contains 3 deprecated functions (getColIndex, getDestinationsByPerson, getDestinationsByPlace) that log warnings on each call. Should be removed after migration period.
tech_debt_findings.fix_suggestion:
1. Search codebase for remaining callers
2. Migrate to *_IDX.* constants
3. Schedule removal for V7.0 milestone
4. Add @deprecated JSDoc tag with removal version

naming.evidence: Excellent consistency across 58 files. Public functions use camelCase (resolvePerson, findCandidates), private functions use trailing underscore (resolvePerson_, findCandidatesByAliasFastPath_), constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (AI_CONFIG, SHEET, FACT_IDX), types use PascalCase (DecisionRule). File naming follows XX_ModuleName.gs convention (00_App.gs through 29_SnapshotTest.gs). Minor inconsistency: some files use 'reproc' prefix (12b_ReviewReprocessor) while others spell out abbreviations.

function_size.evidence: Most functions well under 100-line target. Average function size ~58 lines across Group 2. Excellent examples: processOneRow() (44 lines), executeDecision() (46 lines), handleReview_() (11 lines). Concerns: 4-5 functions exceed 100 lines (runTestMatchDryRun_ ~213L, applyReviewDecision ~162L, resolveAndPersistMerge_ ~158L, fetchDataFromSCGJWD ~190L, runPipelineBatch_ ~150L). ESLint max-lines-per-function set to 200 (warn) which is appropriate compromise for GAS.

comment_jsdoc.evidence: Comprehensive JSDoc documentation on all public functions. Every .gs file includes header block with VERSION, FILE, PURPOSE, DEPENDENCIES, CALLS, EXPORTS TO, SHEETS ACCESSED, TRIGGERS, ARCHITECTURE sections. @param, @return, @private tags consistently applied. Inline comments mix Thai+English appropriately for bilingual team. Version annotations like [V6.0.001], [FIX v003], [PERF-012] provide excellent change tracking.

error_handling.evidence: Strong try-catch coverage on entry points (Law 12 compliance). All menu handlers wrapped with try-catch-logError pattern. LockService used correctly with tryLock(timeout)+finally{releaseLock}. Failsafe patterns in critical paths (logAuditTrail never throws, returns false on failure). Areas for improvement: Some silent catch blocks in 12_ReviewService (intentional for optional data but could use logDebug). Overall error handling maturity high after 18 audit cycles.

consistency.evidence: Highly consistent codebase after 18 audit cycles. Uniform patterns: module headers, function signatures, error handling, cache operations, naming conventions. Prettier configured (single quote, 2-space indent, 120 col width, LF endings). ESLint rules enforced (no-var=error, prefer-const=warn). Minor inconsistencies: some older files retain legacy patterns (99_Legacy), magic number placement varies between inline and AI_CONFIG.

gas_best_practices.evidence: Excellent GAS-specific optimizations: Checkpoint+Auto-Resume pattern (Law 5) in all long-running pipelines, Chunked Cache for >100KB values (Law 4), 3-Layer Cache architecture (RAM→CacheService→Sheet), Batch operations (getValues/setValues not getValue/setValue in loops), LockService with tryLock(timeout) not blocking lock, Time guard (hasTimePassed_) every 100 rows in pipelines, PropertiesService for config/checkpoint/trigger IDs. All 7 hard limits from best practices documented and handled.

security_mindset.evidence: Strong security posture: RBAC with deny-by-default (27_RbacService), PII masking in logs (maskEmail_, maskReviewerEmail_), XSS prevention via escapeHtml() in views, Authorization guards on destructive ops, API keys in HTTP headers not URLs, Formula injection prevention (sanitizeForSheet_), Input validation before writes. Two minor XSS findings (MapAnalytics.html:125, LiveFeed.html:79) need fixing. Auth debug logs could use logDebug instead of logInfo.

maintainability.evidence: Good modularity with clear separation: Core→Master DB→Daily Ops→WebApp→Pipeline Manager. Dependency documentation in every file header. Snapshot tests for refactoring safety (29_SnapshotTest). Legacy code isolated (99_Legacy). Concerns: Several large files (>1000 lines) need further splitting (21_AliasService at 1796L, 18_ServiceSCG at 1228L, 12_ReviewService at 1057L, 24_PipelineManager at 1531L). Some functions still exceed SRP guidelines despite previous refactoring rounds.

top_strengths: Comprehensive JSDoc file headers with dependency maps in all 38 .gs files
Excellent GAS-specific patterns: Checkpoint/Auto-Resume, Chunked Cache, 3-Layer Cache, Batch Operations
Strong security: RBAC, PII masking, XSS prevention, authorization guards on all destructive ops
Consistent naming conventions and code style enforced by ESLint + Prettier
Well-factored Match Engine: 10 main file split into 10b/10d/10e/10f/10g/10h sub-modules
Defensive coding: failsafe patterns, graceful degradation, comprehensive error logging
top_improvements: Split 3-4 god functions exceeding 150 lines (applyReviewDecision, fetchDataFromSCGJWD, runPipelineBatch_, runTestMatchDryRun_)
Move magic numbers/thresholds to AI_CONFIG constants (Thailand bounds, Dice coefficients, distance thresholds)
Fix 2 XSS vulnerabilities in WebApp views (MapAnalytics.html, LiveFeed.html)
Extract duplicated cache invalidation logic to shared utility in 14_Utils.gs

good_examples.why: Perfect SRP example: Orchestrator delegates to 3 focused helpers. Under 50 lines, single responsibility, clear flow.
Excellent defensive design: input validation, whitelist enforcement, fail-safe guarantee. Never breaks caller even on failure.
Production-quality circuit breaker pattern: daily quota tracking, one-time alert deduplication, configurable threshold. Prevents runaway alias creation.
Critical security utility properly escapes all 5 dangerous HTML characters. Extracted once, used consistently across 7+ view files. DRY principle for security.

bad_examples.issue: God function violating SRP - handles validation, 4 decision types, persistence, audit, cache, notifications in single function
bad_examples.fix: Extract to: validateReviewInput() → execute{Merge|Create|Escalate|Ignore}Decision() → persistReviewResult(). Target: each <50 lines

bad_examples.issue: Data inserted into HTML without escaping - potential XSS if matchStatus contains malicious HTML
Hardcoded geographic bounds as inline magic numbers - not configurable, not self-documenting
bad_examples.fix: Define THAILAND_BOUNDS constant in 01_Config.gs: { LAT_MIN: 5.5, LAT_MAX: 20.5, LNG_MIN: 97.5, LNG_MAX: 105.5 }

bad_examples.issue: Same cache invalidation loop duplicated in 2 functions - maintenance burden and DRY violation

code_review_tips.tip_title: Split god function using Strategy pattern
code_review_tips.issue: applyReviewDecision() is 162 lines with switch/case handling 4 decision types plus persistence/audit/cache/notification logic
code_review_tips.why: SRP (Law 2): Single function = Single job. Smaller functions are easier to test, review, and modify without affecting other decision types. Pattern already proven in 10g_MatchRowProcessor.gs

code_review_tips.tip_title: Extract magic numbers to named constant
code_review_tips.issue: Thailand bounding box coordinates hardcoded inline reduce readability and configurability
code_review_tips.why: Law 1 (Clean Code): Self-documenting code. Magic numbers hide intent. Named constants enable reuse (e.g., map boundary display) and easy tuning. Follows AI_CONFIG.GEO_RADIUS_M pattern already in codebase.

code_review_tips.tip_title: Fix XSS vulnerability in Leaflet popup
code_review_tips.issue: Unescaped data bound to HTML in marker popup - potential injection vector
code_review_tips.why: Law 16 (Security-First): Defense-in-depth against XSS. Even though data comes from trusted Google Sheets, output escaping prevents exploitation if data source changes. ViewHelpers.escapeHtml() already available and used in sibling views.

code_review_tips.issue: Dice coefficient thresholds hardcoded as local variables prevent runtime tuning
code_review_tips.why: Configurability without redeployment. Thresholds affect match quality significantly - should be tunable per deployment environment. Enables A/B testing of matching sensitivity.

code_review_tips.tip_title: Extract duplicated cache invalidation to utility
code_review_tips.issue: Identical chunked cache removal loop appears in 2 places - DRY violation
code_review_tips.why: DRY principle: Single implementation means bug fix applies everywhere. Cache key format change only needs one edit. Reduces cognitive load when reading either function.

code_review_tips.tip_title: Refactor pipeline orchestrator using Template Method pattern
code_review_tips.issue: runPipelineBatch_() at ~150 lines mixes initialization, quota checking, loop logic, checkpointing, finalization
code_review_tips.why: Template Method pattern (from lmds-refactor-advisor Recipe 2): Separates orchestration (high-level flow) from worker (low-level details). Each phase independently testable. Matches established pattern in 10_MatchEngine.runMatchEngine_().

code_review_tips.tip_title: Reduce auth logging verbosity in production
code_review_tips.issue: logInfo emits email to SYS_LOG on every WebApp page load - creates log volume and PII exposure
code_review_tips.why: Log hygiene: Production logs should capture anomalies, not normal operations. Auth failures are security events worth logging; successful auth is expected behavior. Reduces SYS_LOG growth rate and PII exposure surface.

code_review_tips.tip_title: Extract dry-run test sub-functions for clarity
code_review_tips.issue: runTestMatchDryRun_() at 213 lines mixes setup, processing loop, result formatting, and sheet output
code_review_tips.why: SRP improvement enables targeted testing: can unit test formatDryRunResults_() with mock data without running full engine. Reduces cognitive load when reviewing test output logic separately from matching logic.

code_review_tips.tip_title: Apply consistent XSS escaping for error display
code_review_tips.issue: Error message inserted via innerHTML without escaping - inconsistent with sibling views
code_review_tips.why: Consistency: All 10 view files should follow same XSS prevention pattern. LiveFeed.html is currently the only view missing escapeHtml on dynamic content. Low risk (internal errors) but zero-cost fix.

code_review_tips.tip_title: Decompose SCG fetch into pipeline stages
code_review_tips.why: Pipeline decomposition enables: (1) Testing fetch without writing, (2) Reusing parse/transform in manual import, (3) Retry logic isolation, (4) Clearer error attribution when stage N fails. Follows Unix pipeline philosophy: each stage does one thing well.

code_review_tips.tip_title: Add debug logging to silent catch blocks
code_review_tips.issue: Silent catch blocks hide potential data corruption in optional JSON fields
code_review_tips.why: Observability: Silent failures in data parsing can indicate schema drift or corruption. Debug-level logging leaves audit trail without polluting production logs or requiring developer intervention to add temporary console.log statements during incident investigation.

not_yet_checked: Live runtime performance (requires execution in Apps Script environment with real data)
Race conditions under concurrent execution (requires load testing with multiple triggers)
GAS 6-minute timeout actual behavior (requires long-running pipeline test)
Memory usage patterns with large datasets (>10K rows in SOURCE sheet)
Google Maps API quota consumption under peak load
UrlFetchApp success rate with actual SCG endpoint (may differ from test environment)
WebApp UI rendering performance with large datasets (browser-side)
Cache hit/miss ratios in production (depends on data distribution)

summary.notes: LMDS V6.0 demonstrates strong code quality after 18 audit cycles. Overall score 78% (Grade B+) reflects mature codebase with consistent patterns, good documentation, and GAS-specific optimizations. 2 P1 issues (XSS in WebApp views) should be fixed before production deployment to users outside trusted network. 9 P2 issues are improvements that reduce technical debt but don't block release. No P0 (data corruption/security critical) issues found - significant achievement for a system of this complexity (34K+ lines, 533 functions, 58 files). Recommendation: CONDITIONAL GO - fix 2 P1 XSS issues first, then proceed with deployment. P2 items can be addressed in subsequent sprints.
